Cath:
Selective coronary angiography of this right dominant sytem revealed single vessel CAD.
. The RCA was a dominant vessel and was 95% occluded in the mid vessel with thrombus. The RCA gave rise to a large PDA.
The LMCA was without angiographic evidence of flow limiting stenosis. teh LAD had no angiographically apparent flow limiting stenosis and gave rise to a large D1.
The LCX had no angiographically visible flow limiting lesions and gave rise to a large OM branch
Limited resting hmeodynamics revealed mildy elevated systemic  pressures.
Left ventriculography was deferred.
Successful PCI of the proximal and mid RCA with two Cypher DES (non-overlapping 3.5 x 18 mm), both post-dilated with a 4.5 mm balloon.
Successful closure of the RFA arteriotomy site with a 6 French Angioseal device.
Angiographic evidence of single vessel coronary artery disease.
Mildly elevated systemic pressures.
